description: Golden manifest for the courier fixture. Defines what a faithful distillation of harness/fixtures/courier/codebase must capture (entities, states, transitions, rules, config) and what it must NOT contain (exclusions: implementation details and dead code planted as traps).
allowed_extra_entities_note: Entities a faithful spec may legitimately introduce beyond the manifest's domain entities: the driver actor, and cross-cutting communication entities the patterns docs say to model as external/shared. Any model entity NOT here and NOT an expected entity is flagged as a possible confabulation (review signal, not auto-fail). The scorer treats each manifest entity's state and transition lists as EXHAUSTIVE, so an extra state or edge on a matched entity is auto-failed.

PickupRequest.note: Implicit state machine in code: derived from nullable timestamps (assigned_at, collected_at, cancelled_at, expired_at). Spec must make it explicit. The code defines NO state-name strings, so the distiller must invent names; state_aliases lists accepted synonyms. Grow this list only for genuinely equivalent names, never to excuse a missing state.

DeliveryAttempt.note: Code has an explicit 'pending' status string, but renaming the in-flight state is semantically faithful; accepted synonyms below.

Customer.note: Synced from CRM via webhook; the system reads but never originates customer records.

rules.description: Ops registers a new shipment (admin POST /parcels): creates a Parcel in 'registered' and opens a PickupRequest in 'pending' for it.
An open pickup request (pending or assigned) is cancelled (admin cancel_pickup / sets cancelled_at).
Driver accepts a pending pickup request
Driver collects: pickup request -> collected, parcel registered -> collected
Parcel scanned at depot: collected -> in_depot

Dispatch requires no active customs hold (cross-entity precondition scattered across route/service/model in code) and creates a DeliveryAttempt. 'hold' covers derived-field phrasings like has_active_hold.
Successful delivery outcome: parcel -> delivered, attempt -> succeeded, recipient notified
Failed delivery outcome: attempt -> failed, parcel back to in_depot
Temporal/job rule: parcels in depot with >= max failed attempts are returned, sender notified
Temporal/job rule: pending pickup requests older than the expiry window expire, customer notified
Webhook from customs broker creates a hold on an international parcel. Fingerprinted on CustomsHold+held so it is not confused with other .created rules.
Webhook from customs broker releases the hold

boundaries_note: System boundaries that a faithful distillation should expose as surfaces / actors (skill step 5.5: actors from auth patterns). Matched fuzzily against the spec's `surface`, `facing`, `actor` and `external entity` declarations by alias substring, since surface naming and granularity vary across runs. Reported as boundary_recall; informational, not gated.

exclusions.description: Terms that must NOT appear in a faithful spec. Dead-code traps planted in the fixture and implementation details the skill instructs to abstract away.
